column1: # amiral butin — embedded price table ($ per token, output-rate model)
# pricing_version: 2026-07-22
# format: pricing_id<TAB>in_usd_per_tok<TAB>out_usd_per_tok<TAB>cache_write<TAB>cache_read<TAB>[effective_from]
# multi-provider from day one; refreshable from LiteLLM but never required.
# dated ids: the platform may report a model id with a trailing -YYYYMMDD
# (e.g. claude-haiku-4-5-20251001). lib/butin/measure.py retries a pricing
# MISS exactly once with that suffix stripped; if the undated id below still
# isn't found, the task stays unmeasurable — never a guessed/neighbouring
# rate. Only ids listed here (dated or not) are ever priced.
# date-aware pricing: the OPTIONAL 6th column, effective_from (ISO
# YYYY-MM-DD), makes MULTIPLE rows per pricing_id legal — one undated BASE
# row (applies from the beginning of time) plus any number of dated cutover
# rows. lib/butin/measure.py picks the rate by the EVENT'S OWN ts (the
# receipt's ts), NEVER by the date the measurement happens to run — history
# is therefore never repriced: the same receipts + transcripts always
# reprice to the same dollar (the REPRODUCIBLE guarantee). A future cutover
# for any model = add one dated row here, zero code changes. A malformed
# effective_from skips that ROW ENTIRELY (never a guessed cutover date);
# pre-existing 5-column (undated) rows keep working unchanged.
claude-opus-4-8
claude-fable-5
# claude-sonnet-5: intro pricing through 2026-08-31 (undated BASE row below
# — also the fallback for any event whose ts predates this table), standard
# pricing from 2026-09-01 (dated cutover row). Cache multipliers follow this
# table's convention: cache_write = 1.25x in, cache_read = 0.10x in.
claude-sonnet-5
claude-sonnet-5
claude-sonnet-4-6
claude-haiku-4-5
gpt-5
gpt-5-mini
gemini-2-5-pro
mistral-large
# GPT-5.6 family (source: openai.com/index/gpt-5-6, GA 2026-07-09; cache: write=1.25x in, read=0.10x in)
gpt-5.6-sol
gpt-5.6-terra
gpt-5.6-luna
